description_long: This is a wooden cabinet with a tall, flat back panel and a slanted writing surface, resembling a lectern or confessional stand. The front features two rectangular doors, each adorned with a carved double-barred cross, suggesting a religious or ceremonial function. The wood grain is visible throughout, giving the object a natural, warm appearance. The cabinet's lower section is boxy and solid, while the upper section is defined by the sloped writing or display surface. There are no visible handles or knobs on the doors, and the object appears to be freestanding. The craftsmanship is simple but sturdy, with a matte finish and no glass or metal elements visible.
description: A wooden cabinet with a tall back, slanted writing surface, and two carved doors featuring double-barred crosses, finished in a natural wood grain.
description_view_0: The back and side of the cabinet are visible, showing the tall, flat rear panel and the boxy lower section.
description_view_1: A side view highlighting the slanted writing surface and the depth of the cabinet's body.
description_view_2: The front view reveals two carved doors with double-barred crosses and the slanted writing surface above.
description_view_3: An angled view from above, showing the writing surface and the tops of the carved doors.
